messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).

No, Socrates did not write the Nicomachean Ethics (尼各馬可倫理學). The Nicomachean Ethics is attributed to Aristotle. Importantly, Socrates left no known extant writings at all — our knowledge of his philosophy comes primarily through the dialogues written by Plato and the accounts of Xenophon. Attributing the Nicomachean Ethics to Socrates would be doubly mistaken: it belongs to Aristotle, and Socrates is not known to have authored any text.
